data_in.description: Retention-time-based feature groups from SimilarRtimeParam grouping
Preprocessed LC-MS feature table with abundances across samples

data_out.description: Count of sub-groups generated by AbundanceSimilarityParam refinement
Pairwise correlation plot for FG.040 showing abundance similarities between features

domain_knowledge: AbundanceSimilarityParam applies Pearson correlation on log2-transformed (or other user-specified) abundance values across samples to split retention-time-based groups; the threshold parameter (here 0.7) determines the minimum correlation coefficient for features to remain grouped.

Filled=TRUE means missing feature abundances (gaps where peaks were not detected in some samples) are imputed before correlation calculation, allowing more robust similarity assessment across all samples.
Features of the same compound are expected to show correlated abundance patterns across samples because they represent co-ionizing ions or adducts of the same molecular entity.
Pairwise correlation plots visualize Pearson correlation coefficients between all pairs of features within a group, with the grouping threshold typically marked to distinguish sub-group assignments.

verify file_format_is: output sub-group count is a single integer value

expert_review: sub-group count matches the reported value from article section describing FG.040 refinement (exact byte-for-byte match with cited result)
expert_review: Review whether AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE) correctly implements log2-transformation of abundances and properly handles filled values during correlation calculation

Assess whether pairwise correlation plot for FG.040 accurately represents inter-feature Pearson or Spearman correlations at the specified threshold and displays expected visual structure (clusters, dendrograms, or heatmap format)
Validate that the sub-grouping logic correctly splits retention-time-based feature groups into sub-groups where pairwise correlations fall below 0.7 threshold after log2 transformation

evidence_snippets.text: [other] When applying AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE) to retention-time-based feature groups, how many feature sub-groups result, and what is the pairwise correlation pattern within the FG.040 feature group?: 'Below we use the `groupFeatures` with the `AbundanceSimilarityParam` to group features with a correlation coefficient higher than 0.7 including both detected and filled-in signal.'

[other] After applying AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE), the 159 features were grouped into 94 feature groups. Within FG.040, pairwise correlation analysis reveals that not all features show correlation above the 0.7 threshold—for example, clear correlation is present between FT273 and FT274, and between FT143 and FT273, but other features within this retention time group show weaker correlations.: 'After feature grouping by abundance correlation, the `r nrow(featureDefinitions(xmse))` features have been grouped into `r length(unique(featureGroups(xmse)))` feature groups. Indeed, correlation'

[methods] Preprocessed LC-MS feature table with abundances across samples: 'The abundance of features (ions) of the same compound should have a similar pattern across samples'

[methods] Pairwise correlation plot for FG.040 showing abundance similarities between features: 'AbundanceSimilarityParam: perform a feature grouping based on correlation of feature abundances (values) across samples'
[intro] xcms: 'The *xcms* R package provides functionality to efficiently preprocess LC-MS (as well as GC-MS and LC-MS/MS) data'
[intro] MsFeatures: 'General MS feature grouping functionality if defined by the `r Biocpkg("MsFeatures")` package'

[discussion] No changelog documenting modifications, bug fixes, or parameter changes to groupFeatures or AbundanceSimilarityParam between article submission and code snapshot (2026-06-15): '_No changelog found._'

cmd: library(xcms); library(MsFeatures); library(pheatmap); rt_groups <- readRDS('{inputs.rt_feature_groups}'); abundances <- read.csv('{inputs.feature_abundances}', row.names=1); param <- AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform='log2', filled=TRUE); refined_groups <- groupFeatures(rt_groups, param); sub_group_counts <- table(refined_groups$featureGroup); cat('Sub-group count:', length(unique(refined_groups$featureGroup)), '\n'); fg040_features <- which(refined_groups$featureGroup == 'FG.040'); if(length(fg040_features) > 0) { fg040_abund <- abundances[fg040_features, ]; fg040_corr <- cor(t(fg040_abund), method='pearson'); png('fg040_correlation_plot.png', width=800, height=800); pheatmap(fg040_corr, main='FG.040 Pairwise Abundance Correlation (log2-transformed)', display_numbers=TRUE, cluster_rows=TRUE, cluster_cols=TRUE); dev.off(); }; saveRDS(data.frame(sub_group_count=sub_group_counts), 'sub_group_summary.rds');
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

expected_outputs: Count of sub-groups generated by AbundanceSimilarityParam refinement
Pairwise correlation plot for FG.040 showing abundance similarities between features
finding: After applying AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE), the 159 features were grouped into 94 feature groups. Within FG.040, pairwise correlation analysis reveals that not all features show correlation above the 0.7 threshold—for example, clear correlation is present between FT273 and FT274, and between FT143 and FT273, but other features within this retention time group show weaker correlations.

task_description: Starting from retention-time-based feature groups, apply groupFeatures with AbundanceSimilarityParam (threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE) to refine groups based on abundance correlation, then reproduce the reported sub-group count and generate the pairwise correlation plot for feature group FG.040.
task_objective: Apply AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, transform=log2, filled=TRUE) to the retention-time-grouped xcms result object and verify the final feature sub-group count and reproduce the pairwise correlation patterns in FG.040.
title: Reproduce abundance-correlation sub-grouping using AbundanceSimilarityParam(threshold=0.7, log2) on faahKO feature groups

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: pheatmap, Count of sub-groups generated by AbundanceSimilarityParam refinement, Pairwise correlation plot for FG.040 showing abundance similarities between features.
workflow_description: 1. Load retention-time-based feature groups (output from SimilarRtimeParam grouping). 2. Apply groupFeatures function with AbundanceSimilarityParam specifying threshold=0.7, transform=log2, and filled=TRUE to perform abundance-correlation-based refinement. 3. Extract sub-group assignments and count total sub-groups produced by the refinement step. 4. Generate a pairwise correlation plot showing abundance correlations across samples for all features in FG.040, visualizing which features cluster together under the abundance similarity criterion.
